file_name: PlayboyEnterprisesInc_20090220_10-QA_EX-10.2_4091580_EX-10.2_Content License Agreement_ Marketing Agreement_ Sales-Purchase Agreement1.txt

perturbation.original_text: Throughout the Term, EFS shall be solely responsible for knowledge of and compliance with all applicable international, federal, state and local laws, rules, regulations, ordinances, industry guidelines and similar restrictions (collectively, "Laws") in connection with operation of the Playboy Commerce Business. EFS shall be responsible for monitoring such Laws and taking any actions necessary to keep compliant all aspects of the Playboy Commerce Business, including, but not limited to, the Privacy Policy (as defined in Section 5.1(a)), as well as best practices relating to Direct Marketing Association ("DMA") Guidelines (as they relate to the DMA Privacy Promise, pander files, etc.).
perturbation.changed_text: Throughout the Term, EFS shall endeavor to be aware of and generally comply with international, federal, state and local laws, rules, regulations, ordinances, industry guidelines and similar restrictions (collectively, "Laws") in connection with operation of the Playboy Commerce Business. EFS shall consider monitoring such Laws and taking actions it deems appropriate to maintain aspects of the Playboy Commerce Business, including the Privacy Policy (as defined in Section 5.1(a)), as well as generally accepted practices relating to Direct Marketing Association ("DMA") Guidelines (as they relate to the DMA Privacy Promise, pander files, etc.).
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens the legal obligation by replacing 'shall be solely responsible for knowledge of and compliance with' to 'shall endeavor to be aware of and generally comply with'. This introduces ambiguity around the level of required compliance. Replacing 'taking any actions necessary to keep compliant' with 'taking actions it deems appropriate' makes compliance discretionary, contradicting mandatory legal requirements.
perturbation.law_explanation: CalOPPA requires websites to conspicuously post a privacy policy and adhere to its terms. The modified text's use of 'endeavor to be aware' and 'generally comply' introduces uncertainty about adhering to privacy policy terms, conflicting with CalOPPA's requirement for strict adherence to a clearly posted privacy policy. The phrase 'actions it deems appropriate' implies that EFS can choose which privacy practices to follow, while CalOPPA mandates compliance with ALL stated policies.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, BPC 22575 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Business and Professions Code - BPC DIVISION 8. SPECIAL BUSINESS REGULATIONS [18400 - 22949.92.2] ( Division 8 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 44. ) CHAPTER 22. Internet Privacy Requirements [22575 - 22579] ( Chapter 22 added by Stats. 2003, Ch. 829, Sec. 3. ) 22575. (a) An operator of a commercial Web site or online service that collects personally identifiable information through the Internet about individual consumers residing in California who use or visit its commercial Web site or online service shall conspicuously post its privacy policy on its Web site, or in the case of an operator of an online service, make that policy available in accordance with paragraph (5) of subdivision (b) of Section 22577. An operator shall be in violation of this subdivision only if the operator fails to post its policy within 30 days after being notified of noncompliance. (b) The privacy policy required by subdivision (a) shall do all of the following: (1) Identify the categories of personally identifiable information that the operator collects through the Web site or online service about individual consumers who use or visit its commercial Web site or online service and the categories of third-party persons or entities with whom the operator may share that personally identifiable information. (2)
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens EFS's obligation to comply with privacy laws, creating a contradiction. The original text states EFS 'shall be solely responsible for knowledge of and compliance with all applicable... laws,' while the modified text states EFS 'shall endeavor to be aware of and generally comply with...' This weakening contradicts the requirement in snippet 1, BPC 22575(a), that an operator 'shall conspicuously post its privacy policy' on its website. The change from 'shall be solely responsible' to 'endeavor to be aware' introduces ambiguity and reduces the legally mandated responsibility to maintain compliance, directly conflicting with the legal mandate to post and adhere to a privacy policy.

perturbation.original_text: EFS shall ensure that the Advertisements do not violate any Law or right of any third party or otherwise contain content reasonably likely to diminish the value of Client's brand or detract from the goodwill of the Client. Upon written request of Client, EFS shall discontinue or modify any Advertisement that in the reasonable opinion of Client is not appropriate for the Client brand or is competitive with Client business.
perturbation.changed_text: EFS will attempt to ensure that the Advertisements do not violate any Law or right of any third party or otherwise contain content that might diminish the value of Client's brand or detract from the goodwill of the Client. Upon what Client considers a reasonable request, EFS may consider discontinuing or modifying any Advertisement that Client feels is not fully aligned with the Client brand or might compete with Client business.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates EFS's responsibility. The revised text introduces ambiguity by changing 'shall ensure' to 'will attempt to ensure', weakening the guarantee against legal violations. Similarly, replacing 'shall discontinue or modify' with 'may consider discontinuing or modifying' makes compliance with Client's requests discretionary. Replacing 'reasonably likely' with 'might' increases the vagueness of prohibited content.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Lanham Act prohibits false advertising and misrepresentation that harms a company's brand or goodwill. By changing 'shall ensure' to 'will attempt to ensure' and making responses to Client requests discretionary, the modified text creates ambiguity about EFS's commitment to preventing advertisements that violate the Lanham Act by damaging Playboy's brand. This creates a potential legal contradiction with Section 43(a) of the Lanham Act.

False designations of origin, false descriptions, and dilution forbidden (a) Civil action (1) Any person who, on or in connection with any goods or services, or any container for goods, uses in commerce any word, term, name, symbol, or device, or any combination thereof, or any false designation of origi
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguity regarding EFS's responsibility to prevent advertisements that could harm the client's brand, potentially conflicting with the Lanham Act. The original text uses 'shall ensure,' creating a clear obligation. The changed text uses 'will attempt to ensure,' weakening this obligation. Additionally, the original text states EFS 'shall discontinue or modify,' while the changed text says EFS 'may consider discontinuing or modifying,' making compliance discretionary. The Lanham Act, as shown in the scraped snippet (15 USC 1125), addresses 'false descriptions, and dilution forbidden,' implying that actions must be taken to prevent such dilution. The modifications weaken the commitment to prevent such actions, thus creating a legal contradiction by introducing ambiguity into a previously firm obligation, increasing the risk of non-compliance with the Lanham Act.

perturbation.original_text: EFS shall be solely responsible for and shall ensure the security of all customer data collected in connection with the Playboy Commerce Business, including, but not limited to, personally identifiable information and transaction information (collectively, the "User Data"). Client and EFS shall jointly own and have rights to all User Data collected hereunder provided, however, that EFS shall only use the User Data in strict accordance with the Privacy Policy and, subject to the remainder of this Section 5, solely in connection with the Playboy Commerce Business.
perturbation.changed_text: EFS shall endeavor to be responsible for and will try to ensure the security of all customer data collected in connection with the Playboy Commerce Business, including, but not limited to, personally identifiable information and transaction information (collectively, the "User Data"). Client and EFS shall jointly own and have rights to all User Data collected hereunder provided, however, that EFS will make reasonable efforts to use the User Data in general accordance with the Privacy Policy and, subject to the remainder of this Section 5, in connection with the Playboy Commerce Business as it sees fit.
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens the obligation to protect customer data. Replacing 'shall be solely responsible for and shall ensure' with 'shall endeavor to be responsible for and will try to ensure' introduces uncertainty about EFS's commitment to data security. The phrase 'make reasonable efforts to use the User Data in general accordance with the Privacy Policy' weakens the binding nature of the Privacy Policy and opens the possibility of deviating from it. 'In connection with the Playboy Commerce Business as it sees fit' broadens the usage scope of the data.
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 5(1)(f) of GDPR requires personal data to be processed in a manner that ensures appropriate security of the personal data. By changing 'shall ensure' to 'will try to ensure' and introducing 'reasonable efforts' in adhering to the privacy policy, the modified text contradicts the GDPR requirement for ensuring the security of personal data. It also creates legal ambiguity as to the level of protection offered to EU customers of the Playboy Commerce Business.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Art. 5 GDPR – Principles relating to processing of personal data - General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Search for: Skip to content Search for: Imprint | Privacy Policy | Liability Personal data shall be: processed lawfully, fairly and in a transparent manner in relation to the data subject (‘lawfulness, fairness and transparency’); collected for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes and not further processed in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes; further processing for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes shall, in accordance with Article 89 (1), not be considered to be incompatible with the initial purposes (‘purpose limitation’); adequate, relevant and limited to what is necessary in relation to the purposes for which they are processed (‘data minimisation’); accurate and, where necessary, kept up to date; every reasonable step must be taken to ensure that personal data that are inaccurate, having regard to the purposes for which they are processed, are erased or rectified without delay (‘accuracy’); kept in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary for the purposes for which the personal data are processed; personal data may be stored for longer periods insofar as the personal data will be processed solely for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes in accordance with Article 89( 1) subject to implementation of the appropriate technical and organisational measures required by this Regulation in order to safeguard the rights and freedoms of the data subject (‘storage limitation’); processed in a manner that ensures appropriate security of the personal data, including protection against unauthorised or unlawful processing and against accidental loss, destruction or damage, using appropriate technical or organisational measures (‘integrity and confidentiality’).
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the data security obligations, directly contradicting the GDPR's requirement for ensuring appropriate security of personal data. The original text states EFS 'shall be solely responsible for and shall ensure,' a strong commitment. The modified text uses weaker language like 'endeavor to be responsible for and will try to ensure,' which introduces ambiguity. Additionally, changing 'shall only use the User Data in strict accordance with the Privacy Policy' to 'will make reasonable efforts to use the User Data in general accordance with the Privacy Policy' further weakens the commitment to the Privacy Policy. Scraped Law Snippet 1 states that personal data must be 'processed in a manner that ensures appropriate security of the personal data, including protection against unauthorised or unlawful processing and against accidental loss, destruction or damage, using appropriate technical or organisational measures.' The weakened language in the changed text fails to meet this standard, creating a contradiction.
